title: José Jiménez Aranda

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2026)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(7 February 1837 – 6 May 1903) was a Spanish painter and brother of the painters [Luis Jiménez Aranda](/wiki/Luis_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda "Luis Jiménez Aranda")[[1]](#cite_note-v711-1) and [Manuel Jiménez Aranda](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda&action=edit&redlink=1 "Manuel Jiménez Aranda (page does not exist)") [[es](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manuel_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda "es:Manuel Jiménez Aranda")].

["José Jiménez Aranda"](https://www.museodelprado.es/en/the-collection/artist/jimenez-aranda-jose/ba04bc6d-6a61-4d53-89de-95b915190660). *Museo Nacional del Prado*. 20 February 2026. Retrieved 20 February 2026.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Spanish painter (1837–1903)

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Jiménez* and the second or maternal family name is  *Aranda*.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["José Jiménez Aranda"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Jos%C3%A9+Jim%C3%A9nez+Aranda%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2026)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Born | (1837-02-07)7 February 1837  [Seville](/wiki/Seville "Seville"), [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") |

**José Jiménez Aranda** (7 February 1837 – 6 May 1903) was a Spanish painter and brother of the painters [Luis Jiménez Aranda](/wiki/Luis_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda "Luis Jiménez Aranda")[[1]](#cite_note-v711-1) and [Manuel Jiménez Aranda](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda&action=edit&redlink=1 "Manuel Jiménez Aranda (page does not exist)") [[es](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manuel_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda "es:Manuel Jiménez Aranda")].

## Biography and works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography and works")]

### Origins and early influences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Origins and early influences")]

He was initiated in his first steps towards becoming a painter by Manuel Cabral and [Eduardo Cano](/wiki/Eduardo_Cano_de_la_Pe%C3%B1a "Eduardo Cano de la Peña").

Gifted at drawing, in 1851 he entered the [Real Academia de Bellas Artes de Santa Isabel de Hungría](/wiki/Real_Academia_de_Bellas_Artes_de_Santa_Isabel_de_Hungr%C3%ADa "Real Academia de Bellas Artes de Santa Isabel de Hungría") (Royal Academy of Fine Arts of Saint Elizabeth of Hungary) in Seville.[[2]](#cite_note-o803-2) In 1868 he studied the collections in the [Museo del Prado](/wiki/Museo_del_Prado "Museo del Prado") (Prado Museum) in Madrid, especially those of [Goya](/wiki/Francisco_de_Goya "Francisco de Goya") and [Velázquez](/wiki/Vel%C3%A1zquez "Velázquez"). In 1867 he travelled to [Jerez de la Frontera](/wiki/Jerez_de_la_Frontera "Jerez de la Frontera") to work as a restorer and designer of stained glass. In 1871 he moved to [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), where he remained for four years, getting to know [Mariano Fortuny](/wiki/Mariano_Fortuny_(painter) "Mariano Fortuny (painter)"), who greatly influenced his paintings.

### Travel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Travel")]

In 1881 he moved to [París](/wiki/Par%C3%ADs "París"), where he studied for nine years, painting works set in the eighteenth century in the style of Fortuny with notable success. In 1890 he moved to Madrid,[[1]](#cite_note-v711-1) painting scenes of everyday life in a more [costumbrista](/wiki/Costumbrismo "Costumbrismo") style.

### Return to his city of birth

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jos%C3%A9_Jim%C3%A9nez_Aranda&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Return to his city of birth")]

The death of his wife and daughter in 1892 resulted in his returning to his city of birth for the rest of his life. There he was appointed member of the [Academy of Fine Arts](/wiki/Academia_de_Bellas_Artes_(Seville) "Academia de Bellas Artes (Seville)"), where he became a teacher, a position he held until his death in 1903. [Daniel Vázquez Díaz](/wiki/Daniel_V%C3%A1zquez_D%C3%ADaz "Daniel Vázquez Díaz"), [Eugenio Hermoso](/wiki/Eugenio_Hermoso "Eugenio Hermoso"), [Ricardo López Cabrera](/w/index.php?title=Ricardo_L%C3%B3pez_Cabrera&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ricardo López Cabrera (page does not exist)"), [Manuel González Santos](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Gonz%C3%A1lez_Santos&action=edit&redlink=1 "Manuel González Santos (page does not exist)") and Sanz Arizmendi were his pupils during this time in [Seville](/wiki/Seville "Seville").

In the last decade of the nineteenth century he frequented the [Alcala de Guadaíra circle of landscape painters](/wiki/Alcal%C3%A1_de_Guadaira_school "Alcalá de Guadaira school"), leaving this genre some examples of "grand master" works (according to [Sorolla](/wiki/Joaqu%C3%ADn_Sorolla "Joaquín Sorolla")).

Among his best known works are: *A Pass in the Bullring* (1880), *Una desgracia* (*A Disaster* – 1890) and *[Slave for Sale](/wiki/Slave_for_sale "Slave for sale")* (c. 1897).

He was also a noted cartoonist and illustrator, producing 689 highlighted drawings for the tercentenary edition of [*Don Quijote*](/wiki/Don_Quixote "Don Quixote") (published 1905).
